prompt: Write nthFromEnd(a, k) that returns the k-th element counting from the end of the array (k = 1 is the last element). Do not use .at().
Write ringGet(a, i) that returns the element at position i, wrapping back to the start when i runs past the end. Assume i is a non-negative integer and a is non-empty.
Write subrange(a, i, j) that returns a new array holding the elements from index i up to but not including j. Do not use .slice.
Write reverseInPlace(a) that reverses the array in place and returns it. Use two pointers walking toward each other; do not use .reverse().
Write isPalindrome(a) that returns true when the array reads the same forward and backward. Use two pointers; do not reverse the array.
Write everyKth(a, k) that returns a new array of every k-th element starting at index 0. Use an index loop with stride k; do not use .filter.
Write window(a, i, k) that returns a new array of the k elements starting at index i. Assume i + k <= a.length. Do not use .slice.
Write maxWindowSum(a, k) that returns the largest sum of any k consecutive elements. Do not re-sum each window from scratch. Assume 1 <= k <= a.length.
Write chunk(a, k) that splits a into consecutive sub-arrays of length k, the last one shorter if the array does not divide evenly.
Write midpoint(lo, hi) that returns the integer index halfway between lo and hi (floored toward lo).
